paper_overview.citation: Vaid, M., Sarma, K., Kala, P. & Gupta, A. The plight of Najafgarh drain in NCT of Delhi, India: assessment of the sources, statistical water quality evaluation, and fate of water pollutants. Environ Sci Pollut Res 29, 90580–90600 (2022). https://doi.org/10.1007/s11356-022-21710-z

data_sources.description: Status of wastewater treatment plants in Najafgarh drain basin
Background data for the location and discharge load of secondary drains

sites.description: Badshahpur & Basai drain. First major drain. These two drains mix before discharging effluents majorly from agricultural, industrial, and residential areas.
sites.source_quote: Badshahpur & Basai drain 28° 29′ 35.69" N 76°56′ 50.80" E First major drain. These two drains mix before discharging effluents majorly from agricultural, industrial, and residential areas

sites.description: Goyla dairy outlet. Underground outlet discharging effluents from Goyla dairy.
sites.source_quote: Goyla dairy outlet 28° 34′ 37.46" N 77° 2' 10.46" E Underground outlet discharging effluents from Goyla dairy

sites.description: Palam drain. Discharging effluents majorly from residential areas.
sites.source_quote: Palam drain 28° 34′ 46.10" N 77° 2' 19.56" E Discharging effluents majorly from residential areas

sites.description: Mungeshpur drain. Discharging effluents majorly from agricultural and residential areas.
sites.source_quote: Mungeshpur drain 28° 37′ 34.78" N 77° 1' 16.07" E Discharging effluents majorly from agricultural and residential areas

sites.description: Keshopur STP outlet. Discharging effluents from Keshopur sewage treatment plant.
sites.source_quote: Keshopur STP outlet 28° 39′ 14.20" N 77° 4' 42.13" E Discharging effluents from Keshopur sewage treatment plant

sites.description: Basaidarapur drain. Underground outlet discharging effluents majorly from industrial and residential areas.
sites.source_quote: Basaidarapur drain 28° 39′ 30.45" N 77° 7' 29.17" E Underground outlet discharging effluents majorly from industrial and residential areas

sites.description: Kanhaiya Nagar drain. Western Yamuna Canal discharging Yamuna River water contaminated due to discharges majorly from residential and industrial areas.
sites.source_quote: Kanhaiya Nagar drain 28° 40′ 34.78" N 77° 10' 4.98" E Western Yamuna Canal discharging Yamuna River water contaminated due to discharges majorly from residential and industrial areas

sites.description: Supplementary drain. Discharging effluents majorly from residential and industrial areas.
sites.source_quote: Supplementary drain 28° 42′ 42.53″ N 77° 13' 38.34" E Discharging effluents majorly from residential and industrial areas

sites.description: Sampling point on Najafgarh drain, located approximately 500 m upstream to a selected secondary drain.
sites.source_quote: water quality analysis was conducted for twenty-five sampling locations comprising fifteen points on the Najafgarh drain (N1–N15)... In the Najafgarh drain, sampling points were purposely located at approximately 500 m upstream and downstream locations to each selected secondary drain so as to find the effect of that particular secondary drain on the Najafgarh drain water quality.

sites.description: Sampling point on Yamuna River, upstream to the Najafgarh drain outfall.
sites.source_quote: and two points in the Yamuna River (Y1-Y2)... The two points in the Yamuna River were then selected on a similar basis, i.e., upstream and downstream locations to the Najafgarh drain outfall.

sites.description: Sampling point on Yamuna River, downstream to the Najafgarh drain outfall.
sites.source_quote: and two points in the Yamuna River (Y1-Y2)... The two points in the Yamuna River were then selected on a similar basis, i.e., upstream and downstream locations to the Najafgarh drain outfall.

extraction_notes: The paper mentions 25 sampling locations in total, comprising 15 points on the Najafgarh drain (N1–N15), 8 points on secondary drains (S1-S8), and 2 points in the Yamuna River (Y1-Y2). Table 2 provides detailed information for S1-S8. Descriptions for N and Y sites are derived from the text. Coordinates for N1-N15 and Y1-Y2 sites are not explicitly provided in tabular format, only shown on a map (Figure 1). Therefore, latitude and longitude are null for these sites. The paper states 'physicochemical parameters (n = 16), microbiological parameter (n = 1), and heavy metal concentrations (n=8)'. The 'Reagent preparation and analysis' section lists 14 physicochemical parameters (TEMP, pH, EC, SAL, TDS, TURB, DO, BOD, COD, NN, PHOS, TA, Cl-, TSS) plus Na and K as 'other metals', making it 16. Total coliforms is 1 microbiological. Heavy metals Cr, Pb, Fe, Ni, Mn, Zn, Cd, Cu are 8. This matches the total count of 25 parameters.
